skills.title: Onboarding v1 to v2 migration
skills.description: Migrate an existing integration from the deprecated v1 onboarding API to the current onboarding API, using the canonical hub, the step-by-step migration guide, and the field-mapping reference.
prohibitions: Do not recommend the v1 onboarding mutations or patterns: they are deprecated and will be removed at the end of September 2026.
Do not propose a new integration on v1; always direct new and migrating integrations to the current onboarding API via /accounts/guides/onboarding/migrate-api.

skills.title: Plan 2 audience routing (Dashboard vs API)
skills.description: For a task that can be done both from the Swan Dashboard and through the API, surface both audience-specific paths. Each parent task page links its 'From the Dashboard' (partner-ops) and 'Using the API' (developer) children via a counterpart link.
prohibitions: Do not answer a dual-audience task with only one path: when a parent task has both a 'From the Dashboard' and a 'Using the API' child, surface both, not just one.
Do not collapse the two children into a single generic answer; preserve the audience split (partner-ops vs developer) and link each child explicitly.

skills.title: API best practices
skills.description: Apply Swan's canonical API conventions for pagination, authentication, idempotency, error and rejection handling, and webhooks instead of reimplementing them inline.
prohibitions: Do not inline pagination boilerplate; link to and follow the canonical pagination page (/build/using-api/pagination).
Do not duplicate consent or eligibility logic in application code; rely on the documented API behaviour for authentication and errors/rejections.

skills.title: KYC and partner onboarding
skills.description: Route KYC and partner-onboarding questions through the canonical country-requirements hub, proof-of-address and onboarding-requirements references, and the 'Become a partner' regulatory pages (licence, ORIAS, protections).
prohibitions: Do not link old or standalone company-registration country pages without routing through the canonical country-requirements hub (/accounts/reference/onboarding/country-requirements).
Do not state country KYC requirements from memory; cite the per-country requirement page for the relevant country.
